description_long: This chapel features a steeply pitched, dark brown gabled roof with prominent spires at each corner, evoking a Gothic Revival style. The facade includes a central arched entrance with a small staircase, flanked by stone columns and decorative arches. Large, multi-paned windows with pointed arches are visible on the side elevations, allowing natural light to enter the interior. The structure is primarily constructed from stone and wood, with weathered textures suggesting age and historical significance. Ornamental details such as finials and buttresses enhance the verticality and grandeur of the building. The chapel sits on a raised stone platform, further emphasizing its prominence in an urban or park setting.
description: A Gothic Revival chapel with a steep gabled roof, pointed arches, and ornate stone and wood detailing. Large arched windows and spires accentuate its historic and solemn appearance.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main arched entrance, central staircase, and symmetrical spires on either side of the gabled roof.
description_view_1: The left side view highlights the large, pointed-arch windows and the extended stone platform beneath the structure.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals a simpler gabled wall with minimal openings and the continuation of the spires at the roof corners.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, displaying the same large arched windows and architectural details.
attribute_entrance_door_design: The main entrance door is arched, likely made of heavy wood, and framed by stone columns and decorative molding. It is dark in color and features subtle ornamental carvings, consistent with Gothic Revival design.
